enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his first real ride on his new Kawasaki KLX 400 SR dirt bike around Boulder, CO, including running out of gas twice, getting help from a county utility worker, pulling an accidental wheelie, and reflections on motorcycle safety and the adrenaline risk of riding. He also touches on plans for his website/YouTube channel and notes his body is rejecting oatmeal for breakfast.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: motorcycles are genuinely dangerous and impulsive personalities should not ride them — people don't realize how close to wrecking they are
won't lend it to his brother because his brother is too impulsive and will eventually crash
they complement each other perfectly and motorcycling won't replace push biking — they serve different needs
his body is rejecting it and he probably can't eat it anymore, at least not with chocolate

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday was a good day to go out on the motorbike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He first went to his mom's house to show her the bike, but she wasn't there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He drove up to get gas because he didn't know how much gas was left in the bike, as there's no gas gauge.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The gas station he usually goes to was gutted and had no pumps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He didn't want to turn around and go to another gas station due to traffic and lack of preparation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He decided to take dirt roads to get gas, which was 13 miles away.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He ran out of gas about 10 miles away from home on dirt roads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He tried to push, walk, and coast the bike down hills to keep going.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A Boulder County utility truck driver stopped and gave him some gas, citing a 'carton rule' about helping motorbikers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The truck driver mentioned a reserve tank, which Kyle didn't know about.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He ran out of gas again halfway home because he didn't fill it up enough.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had to push the bike up a steep hill, which was a workout.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He remembered the reserve tank and used it to get to a gas station.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He filled up the bike with $6.50 worth of gas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He drove back to explore dirt roads and had fun.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His Kawasaki KLX 400 SR is a dirt bike version of the Suzuki DRZ 400.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The bike is supposed to top out at 95 mph, but it stopped accelerating at 81 mph on a dirt road.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The bike accelerates quickly from 50 to 81 mph.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The bike fishtails on dirt roads when accelerating, which can change trajectory dangerously.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He gained a new perspective on motorcycles after riding this bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can see how people get addicted to pushing the limits on motorbikes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His brother had friends who rode motorbikes, but many of them broke their backs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He realizes how close to wrecking people often are on motorbikes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has to be careful not to get overexcited while riding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He did a California stop at a dirt road crossing and ended up pulling a wheelie unintentionally.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He feels that riding a motorbike is like being at one with the machine, but it's also unnaturally dangerous.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He compares riding a motorbike to strapping razor-sharp claws on a tiger, calling it a death wish for impulsive people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wouldn't loan his motorbike to his impulsive brother.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He initially worried that riding a motorbike would make him lose interest in push biking and get fat.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He realized that motorbiking and push biking are totally different and complement each other.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Motorbiking helps him explore trails and terrain, which makes push biking more enjoyable later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got microscopic crickets for his lizard and a new kind of worm calcium.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He tried to sync up perfect files for MP3 but didn't finish.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to work on finishing files and his website tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to get started on his YouTube page soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can't eat oatmeal with chocolate for breakfast anymore because it makes him gag.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finds it weird that his body rejects oatmeal, even though it's healthy.
